refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical subsystems for speed; minimize teleoperation latency via low-latency network link; design safety services with non-blocking async checks.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling and real-time adaptive control to maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under varying site conditions.
DP-C: Allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems; enforce budget via design reviews and integration testing.
DP-D: Establish telemetry-driven feedback loop to continuously monitor p95 cycle time and trigger corrective actions when threshold is approached.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Allocate explicit cycle time budget across vehicle subsystem, teleoperation subsystem, and safety services; enforce via design reviews and integration testing. Establish continuous telemetry-driven monitoring of p95 cycle time with feedback to operations and engineering for corrective action.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time is coupled to hydraulic response, load mass, and terrain (site conditions) — requires site characterization.
Teleoperation latency is coupled to network link quality and operator skill — requires network SLA and operator training.
Safety services latency is coupled to safety criticality level — high criticality may require synchronous checks that impact cycle time.
Telemetry reporting overhead is coupled to network bandwidth — must not saturate link.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via explicit budget allocation and interface contracts. Safety criticality (high) is a key constraint: safety checks must be non-blocking or pre-computed to avoid cycle time penalty. Recommend decoupling safety validation from real-time cycle path via offline pre-flight checks and async monitoring.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable given vehicle hardware, network latency, and safety constraints. Risk: If CT_target is too aggressive, cycle time KPI may be unachievable without safety compromise.
Assumption: Site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) are well-characterized and stable. Risk: Highly variable site conditions may cause p95 to exceed target despite optimal design.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure is available and reliable. Risk: Telemetry loss or corruption prevents real-time monitoring and corrective action.
Assumption: Operator skill and network link quality are consistent. Risk: Poor operator or network degradation can degrade cycle time independent of system design.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly define cycle time budget allocation (e.g., vehicle: 60%, teleoperation: 20%, safety: 10%, margin: 10%) and acceptance criteria for each subsystem. Refine DP-D to specify telemetry KPI thresholds, alert triggers, and escalation procedures for cycle time exceedances.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for cycle time KPI achievement. Owns the cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Owns the telemetry-driven monitoring and feedback loop.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load start time, travel start/end time, dump start/end time, p95 aggregate) to external monitoring/analytics systems. Frequency: per-cycle or aggregated per shift.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task assignments (load location, dump location, cycle priority) from Site Manager or autonomous planner. Tasking may include cycle time constraints or preferences.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services (e.g., emergency stop, hazard detection) that may interrupt or delay cycle execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry transmission. Network latency is a direct component of cycle time budget.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of cycle time KPI. Cycle time is a system-level emergent property that depends on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution owns the budget allocation, monitoring, and corrective action logic. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry reporting and task/alert interfaces cross boundary.

Vehicle owns the mechanical/hydraulic cycle execution (load, travel, dump). However, cycle time KPI is not solely a Vehicle responsibility — it depends on teleoperation latency and safety constraints. If assigned to Vehicle alone, would require Vehicle to own teleoperation and safety logic, causing high diffusion and coupling.

Teleoperation owns operator command latency and network link utilization. However, cycle time KPI depends equally on Vehicle performance and safety constraints. Assigning to Teleoperation would require ownership of Vehicle and Safety Services, causing high diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety validation and alert generation. Cycle time is not a primary Safety Services responsibility. Assigning cycle time KPI to Safety Services would create conflict of interest (safety vs. speed) and require ownership of Vehicle and Teleoperation, causing very high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that cannot be achieved by any single subsystem in isolation. (2) NGHE Solution owns the integration logic, budget allocation, and monitoring/feedback loop. (3) Containment is high (95%) with minimal diffusion (5%) — only telemetry, tasking, and safety alert interfaces cross the boundary. (4) Ownership is clear and unambiguous: NGHE Solution is responsible for meeting the KPI, and has authority to allocate budget and enforce constraints across subsystems.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may lack direct control over Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services if they are owned by different teams. Mitigation: Establish clear interface contracts and SLAs for cycle time budget allocation.
Risk: Telemetry infrastructure may not be available or reliable, preventing real-time monitoring. Mitigation: Implement fallback monitoring via manual logging or post-hoc analysis.
Risk: Site conditions may vary unpredictably, causing p95 to exceed CT_target despite optimal design. Mitigation: Implement adaptive control and site characterization procedures.

Risk: Safety criticality (high) may require synchronous safety checks that block cycle execution. Mitigation: Decouple safety validation from real-time cycle path via offline pre-flight checks and async monitoring.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the ownership and governance model for cycle time KPI. Define explicit cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services (e.g., via design review gates). Establish SLAs for each subsystem's contribution to cycle time. Define escalation procedures for cycle time exceedances and corrective action triggers.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR clearly specifies the cycle time KPI (p95 ≤ CT_target) and measurement method (telemetry). Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) directly addresses the FR by: (1) allocating cycle time budget across subsystems to ensure p95 target is achievable, (2) establishing continuous monitoring to detect and correct exceedances. DP is verifiable via field trials and telemetry analysis. No logical gaps or contradictions.

diffusion_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may not have direct control over Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services subsystems if they are independently owned. Mitigation: Establish clear interface contracts and SLAs.
Risk: Telemetry reporting overhead may saturate Network Link if not carefully managed. Mitigation: Implement telemetry sampling and compression strategies.
Risk: Cycle time budget allocation may conflict with other system objectives (e.g., safety, cost, reliability). Mitigation: Establish trade-off analysis and design review gates.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI ownership. Containment is high (95%) with minimal diffusion. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and manageable. No evidence of high diffusion or architectural misalignment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI is directly measurable via telemetry (load/travel/dump timestamps). p95 can be computed from telemetry logs. Verification is feasible via field trials under representative duty cycle. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) is clear and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution, which owns the budget allocation and monitoring logic. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through 002.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine cycle time budget allocation (e.g., Vehicle: 60%, Teleoperation: 20%, Safety: 10%, margin: 10%) and document in design specification.
Define telemetry KPI thresholds and alert triggers (e.g., if p95 > 0.95 * CT_target, escalate to engineering).
Establish site characterization procedure to validate CT_target achievability under actual site conditions.
Define safety criticality mitigation strategy to ensure high safety criticality does not compromise cycle time KPI.

decision.justification: Refined FR-NGHE-002 is well-defined and verifiable. Selected DP (budget allocation + telemetry monitoring) is sound and addresses the FR. Assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate with high containment (95%) and minimal diffusion (5%). However, the FR is not atomic — it decomposes into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-002.1 through 002.4) that require further refinement and DP selection. Recommend proceeding to the next decomposition level to detail the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services contributions to cycle time KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below CT_target.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time metrics via telemetry interface.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable given vehicle hardware, network latency, and safety constraints.
Site conditions (terrain, load mass, distance) are well-characterized and stable.
Telemetry infrastructure is available and reliable.
Operator skill and network link quality are consistent.
Safety criticality (high) can be managed via non-blocking async checks or offline pre-flight validation.
Cycle time budget allocation across subsystems is feasible and does not create unacceptable trade-offs.
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be unachievable without safety compromise. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study and trade-off analysis.
Risk: Site conditions may vary unpredictably, causing p95 to exceed target. Mitigation: Implement adaptive control and site characterization.
Risk: Telemetry infrastructure may not be available or reliable. Mitigation: Implement fallback monitoring.
Risk: Safety criticality may require synchronous checks that block cycle execution. Mitigation: Decouple safety validation from real-time cycle path.
Risk: Cycle time budget allocation may conflict with other system objectives. Mitigation: Establish trade-off analysis and design review gates.
evidence_needed: Field trial data demonstrating p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under representative duty cycle.
Telemetry logs showing cycle time distribution and p95 computation.
Site characterization data (terrain, load mass, distance, environmental conditions).
Network latency measurements and SLA documentation.
Safety validation procedures and non-blocking implementation evidence.
Cycle time budget allocation documentation and design review records.
Operator training and skill assessment records.
